attributes.description: Alerts when host CPU utilisation exceeds 80%. Hosts above 80% cause VM performance degradation.
attributes.name: [vSphere OTel] Host CPU utilization high

investigation_guide.blob:
## vSphere host CPU utilization high

### What fired
`vcenter.host.cpu.utilization > 80%` during the window.

### Why it matters
Saturated ESXi hosts cause CPU-ready time on VMs and performance degradation. DRS should normally rebalance, but heavy spikes slip through.

### Triage
1. Check DRS settings and cluster capacity.
2. Identify CPU-heavy VMs.

### Remediation
- Migrate VMs via vMotion.
- Add hosts.

### Tuning
- 80% threshold; 90% for more tolerant envs.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-vcenterreceiver.otel-*
// Host CPU utilization: healthy <80%; unhealthy >90%
// Adjust threshold (e.g. 80 or 90) to suit environment
| WHERE vcenter.host.cpu.utilization IS NOT NULL AND vcenter.host.cpu.utilization > 80
| STATS utilization_pct = MAX(vcenter.host.cpu.utilization) BY vcenter.datacenter.name, vcenter.cluster.name, vcenter.host.name
| SORT utilization_pct DESC
| LIMIT 20
